references.title: Age effects on mouse and human B cells
The generation of memory B cells is maintained, but the antibody response is not, in the elderly after repeated influenza immunizations
Glycans are a novel biomarker of chronological and biological ages
references.container-title: The Journals of Gerontology. Series A, Biological Sciences and Medical Sciences
references.container-title-short: J Gerontol A Biol Sci Med Sci

references.title: Prevalence of monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance
references.container-title: The New England Journal of Medicine

paragraphs.text: Aging reshapes humoral immunity rather than simply lowering all antibody production. In mouse and human B cells, aging has been linked to reduced immunoglobulin class switching, lower activation-induced cytidine deaminase, and impaired affinity maturation, providing a mechanistic frame for weaker class-switched antibody responses

This defect is visible in vaccination studies. After repeated influenza immunizations, elderly adults can maintain memory B-cell generation while failing to maintain the antibody response, suggesting that immunoglobulin output and quality may diverge from memory-cell counts

Immunoglobulins also carry age information in their structure and clonal distribution. IgG N-glycan profiles have been reported as biomarkers of chronological and biological age, linking antibody Fc biology to aging-associated inflammatory phenotypes
. At the clonal end of the spectrum, monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance is defined by a circulating monoclonal immunoglobulin and becomes more prevalent with age

claims.claim: Aging is linked to reduced immunoglobulin class switching, lower activation-induced cytidine deaminase, and impaired affinity maturation in mouse and human B-cell biology.
claims.note: PubMed abstract states that aging decreases Ig class switching, AID, E47, and affinity maturation in elderly vaccine responses.

claims.claim: After repeated influenza immunizations, elderly adults can maintain memory B-cell generation while the antibody response is not maintained.
claims.note: Article title and abstract directly support the contrast between memory B-cell generation and antibody response.

claims.claim: IgG N-glycan profiles can serve as biomarkers of chronological and biological age.
claims.note: Study title and abstract directly frame glycans, including IgG glycosylation, as biomarkers of chronological and biological age.

claims.claim: Monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance reflects a circulating monoclonal immunoglobulin state whose prevalence increases with age.
claims.note: NEJM prevalence study defines MGUS by monoclonal protein criteria and reports age-stratified prevalence.
